title: Texas Secretary of State – LLC Overview
bullets: Choose a unique business name that includes an LLC designator; check name availability with the Secretary of State
File a Certificate of Formation – Limited Liability Company (Form 205) with the Texas Secretary of State
The Certificate of Formation must satisfy the Texas Business Organizations Code (TBOC), Title 3, Chapter 101 requirements
Designate a registered agent and registered office in Texas as required by TBOC (Title 1, Chapter 5)
Draft a Company Agreement (operating agreement) to govern internal affairs and ownership structure (strongly recommended)
Maintain statutory records (e.g., membership records) after formation and keep them up to date

title: Texas Business Organizations Code – Title 3, Chapter 101
bullets: Chapter 101 contains core provisions governing Texas limited liability companies
Certificate of Formation content and organizational structure must align with Chapter 101

title: Texas Business Organizations Code – Title 1, Chapter 5
bullets: Texas LLCs must appoint and continuously maintain a registered agent and registered office within the state
Service of process and official correspondence are delivered to the registered agent

title: Texas Comptroller – Franchise Tax Obligations
bullets: Most Texas LLCs have franchise tax responsibilities with the Comptroller and must file required reports
Ongoing compliance includes timely franchise tax filings and recordkeeping in accordance with Texas requirements
